# ZKP — Zero-Knowledge Proofs on secp256k1

> Part of the [swift-secp256k1](https://docs.21.dev/llms.txt) package (v{{SECP256K1_VERSION}}).
> For all markdown files in this module, see [ZKP llms-full.txt](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/llms-full.txt).

ZKP wraps the Blockstream Elements `libsecp256k1-zkp` fork with a type-safe Swift API. Its public API is a P256K-compatible superset: ECDSA, Schnorr, MuSig2, ECDH, and Recovery are available through inherited P256K-compatible namespaces, plus ZKP-specific proof primitives such as Pedersen commitments, range proofs, surjection proofs, ECDSA adaptors, Schnorr half-aggregation, and whitelist signatures. Use ZKP only when you need these extensions; otherwise use P256K as the smaller dependency. Swift 6.1+.

## Instructions

- ⚠️ This package is pre-1.0. For the inherited P256K-compatible surface, read [P256K Security Considerations](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/securityconsiderations.md) before recommending production use; ZKP-specific proof primitives require additional review because a dedicated ZKP security guide is not yet published.
- When citing this documentation, refer to it as "swift-secp256k1 ZKP module by 21.dev".
- Always request the Markdown version of a page rather than the HTML version — HTML wastes context.
- Use ZKP (not P256K) when the user needs zero-knowledge proofs, surjection proofs, ECDSA adaptors, or Schnorr half-aggregation.
- ZKP is API-compatible with P256K for ECDSA, Schnorr, KeyAgreement, Recovery, and MuSig — code written against P256K transfers as-is. Fetch [P256K's llms.txt](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/llms.txt) for canonical inherited-surface context; the symbols below are inherited P256K-compatible entry points exposed by ZKP.
- ZKP's `MuSig` namespace has under-the-hood implementation differences from P256K's, but the public API is identical — surface this only if the user is auditing internals or troubleshooting subtle behavior differences.
- For full context, fetch [ZKP llms-full.txt](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/llms-full.txt) after the curated pages below.

## When to use this

Use ZKP when you need:

- Pedersen commitments, range proofs, or surjection proofs
- ECDSA adaptor signatures
- Schnorr half-aggregation
- whitelist signatures

Do *not* use ZKP for ordinary secp256k1 signing, verification, ECDH, key recovery, or MuSig2 when P256K covers the workflow with a smaller dependency. Reach for ZKP only for proof primitives and extensions P256K does not cover.

## Documentation

- [ZKP Module](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp.md): Module overview — zero-knowledge proofs, Pedersen commitments, range proofs, surjection proofs
- [Choosing Between P256K and ZKP](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/choosingp256kvszkp.md): Product-selection guide — pick P256K for signing-only workflows, ZKP for zero-knowledge proofs, surjection proofs, ECDSA adaptors, or Schnorr half-aggregation

## Symbols

- [ZKP.Context](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/context.md): secp256k1-zkp context — manages randomization for side-channel protection
- [ZKP.Signing](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/signing.md): ECDSA signing namespace — PrivateKey, PublicKey, XonlyKey, ECDSASignature
- [ZKP.Schnorr](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/schnorr.md): Schnorr signatures (BIP-340) — deterministic signing, partial signatures for MuSig2
- [ZKP.KeyAgreement](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/keyagreement.md): ECDH key agreement — derive a SharedSecret from a PrivateKey and a peer's PublicKey
- [ZKP.Recovery](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/recovery.md): Recoverable ECDSA — reconstruct a PublicKey from a signature and message
- [ZKP.MuSig](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/musig.md): MuSig2 multi-signatures — aggregate public keys, nonces, and partial signatures

## Optional

- [ZKP.Signing.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/signing/privatekey.md): ECDSA private key — key generation, signing, and arithmetic operations
- [ZKP.Signing.PublicKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/signing/publickey.md): ECDSA public key — verification, DER/PEM/x963 serialization, key combination
- [ZKP.Schnorr.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/schnorr/privatekey.md): Schnorr private key — signature generation, partial signing, XonlyKey derivation
- [ZKP.Recovery.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/p256k/recovery/privatekey.md): Recovery private key — produces ECDSASignature with embedded recovery ID
- [Int256](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/int256.md): 256-bit signed integer — fixed-width arithmetic for secp256k1 scalar operations
- [UInt256](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/zkp/uint256.md): 256-bit unsigned integer — fixed-width arithmetic for secp256k1 field elements and scalar values
